response.justification: While the candidate has strong general software development experience spanning 13+ years and some C/Linux background, the resume is missing critical required qualifications. Most significantly, there is no mention of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly required. Additionally, the candidate lacks demonstrated expertise in required security technologies (encryption, authentication systems, attack detection) and embedded networking (Layer 2/3, network switches, SNMP, etc.). Recent career trajectory focuses on full-stack web development rather than embedded systems or security.
strengths: 13+ years of software development experience across diverse domains including embedded systems, mobile, and low-level systems programming
Proficiency with C/C++ and systems-level work demonstrated at National Instruments with embedded development experience
Strong Linux expertise with hands-on infrastructure experience (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab administration)
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency listed (resume shows only English fluent, Romanian fluent, German basic) — this is a required qualification
Complete absence of security domain expertise: no mention of encryption, MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, authentication systems, or attack detection technologies
Recent 5-year career trajectory focused on full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, GraphQL); C experience dates to 2014-2015 and is over a decade old

key_factors.factor: No security domain expertise or background
Recent work focuses on web, not embedded networking
